hero.headline: AI memory format versioning, demonstrated — PMF v0.1 → v0.2 in one command. 100% fidelity, zero external calls.

problem.body: Every AI memory format that ships will need to evolve. ChatGPT changes its export shape; vendor-neutral formats add fields; consumers wake up to schema breakage. The field has spent zero serious time defining how memory formats should version. Add a field, ship a doc, hope nothing downstream cared about that exact shape.

proposal.body: format-survives-upgrades does two things in one repo: it publishes a vendor-neutral versioning rules spec for AI memory formats (additive minor bumps, forward-compat consumers, provenance for migrated docs, stable-id stability under additive change), AND it demonstrates those rules on a real format — PMF v0.2 with a working v0.1 → v0.2 migration CLI. 100% migration fidelity verified on 30 hand-built samples, zero external network calls, three byte-identical runs.

whyNow.body: Plan 5 (context-portability) shipped PMF v0.1 last week. Plan 4 (memorystore) shipped a consumer. The format is real, in use, and about to need a second version. The right moment to plant versioning rules is when there's exactly one format that uses them — credible, testable, not theoretical.

about.body: Built by Aiden — solo developer working through a portfolio of AI primitives in 2026. format-survives-upgrades is one of several exploratory bets; see the hub for the others. This is alpha — expect rough edges, and please file issues.
